title: iptables: Couldn't load match `comment': ENOENT
body:
Seen twice by the multiarch team, on aarch64:
```
[+0025s] not ok 4 podman images - history output
[+0025s] # (from function `die' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 448,
[+0025s] #  from function `run_podman' in file /usr/share/podman/test/system/helpers.bash, line 221,
[+0025s] #  in test file /usr/share/podman/test/system/010-images.bats, line 64)
[+0025s] #   `run_podman run --name ignore-me --name my-container $IMAGE true' failed with status 127
[+0025s] # # podman rm --all --force
[+0025s] # # podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
[+0025s] # # podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
[+0025s] # quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 d47f52704fad
[+0025s] # # podman run --name ignore-me --name my-container quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 true
[+0025s] # time="2021-10-21T14:49:45-04:00" level=error msg="error loading cached network config: network \"podman\" not found in CNI cache"
[+0025s] # time="2021-10-21T14:49:45-04:00" level=warning msg="falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk"
[+0025s] # time="2021-10-21T14:49:46-04:00" level=error msg="Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container 077c31e10e9852f98f2608362c64341405eb87a2cfcd7b55c0b3ca75e18183a1: error removing pod my-container_my-container from CNI network \"podman\": running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s 10.88.0.2 -j CNI-d998ed0eba2a33f76a21b83d -m comment --comment name: \"podman\" id: \"077c31e10e9852f98f2608362c64341405eb87a2cfcd7b55c0b3ca75e18183a1\" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory\n\nTry `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information.\n"
[+0025s] # Error: error configuring network namespace for container 077c31e10e9852f98f2608362c64341405eb87a2cfcd7b55c0b3ca75e18183a1: error adding pod my-container_my-container to CNI network "podman": running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -C CNI-d998ed0eba2a33f76a21b83d -d 10.88.0.2/16 -j ACCEPT -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "077c31e10e9852f98f2608362c64341405eb87a2cfcd7b55c0b3ca75e18183a1" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory
[+0025s] # 
[+0025s] # Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information.
[+0025s] # [ rc=127 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
This is being reported as a flake, because there appears to be one successful run on aarch64 in the past. (But it was waived, so I can't tell if this test is the one that was waived). This makes no sense to me as a flake, it seems like the sort of thing that is 100% a configuration error such as a missing `xt_comment` module, but I will leave it tagged as flake for now.

Versions:
```
iptables-legacy-libs-1.8.7-13.fc35.aarch64
iptables-libs-1.8.7-13.fc35.aarch64
iptables-nft-1.8.7-13.fc35.aarch64
nftables-1:0.9.9-3.fc35.aarch64
```
Reference link: https://gitlab.com/cki-project/kernel-tests/-/issues/799

@mheon @Luap99 is there anything that immediately springs to mind with this?

comments.body: No idea, I agree that this is a config problem. Switching to the legacy iptables should work as workaround but I guess this is not desired for testing.

When we've seen these before, it's usually a bug in `iptables` itself. `go-iptables` relies upon specific exit code being set to determine what exactly is going on in the iptables backend; when we see this it's usually a result of an incorrect exit code being set by iptables, so go-iptables interprets as a general error what is actually an ENOENT that would be handled sanely.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

No activity on the [reference issue](https://gitlab.com/cki-project/kernel-tests/-/issues/799). No instances in my cirrus flake archive. Closing on the assumption that this was a misconfiguration or transient error.
